reasoning:
Let me think through this translation carefully.

## Step 1: Imagining Literal Implementation

If we tried to implement this exactly: People would gather in community halls to "perform" their retirement contributions before witnesses. We'd have professional "Tension Keepers" tracking not money but some vague "dramatic weight." Retirement timing would be determined by consultants reading... narrative readiness?

Obviously absurd. But something interesting is hiding here.

## Step 2: Identifying Magical Elements

| Magical Element | What It Does | Our Physics Equivalent? |
|-----------------|--------------|------------------------|
| Tension Ledger tracking "dramatic weight" | Measures effort/struggle, not dollars | Proportional sacrifice tracking (% of income, not absolute $) |
| Contribution Ceremonies creating force | Public commitment shapes outcome | Commitment devices + witnessed pledges (psychology research confirms these work) |
| Third Act narrative obligation | Reality *must* complete your story | Collective legal/social obligation backed by pooled resources |
| Timing Guilds reading arcs | Detect when transformation is ready | Multi-signal readiness detection (health, burnout, career, readiness markers) |
| Soliloquy shaping reality | Speaking intentions makes them true | Public commitment increases follow-through by 65%+ (Cialdini's research) |
| Exit Performance quality matters | Good transition → good elderhood | Actually true: phased retirement correlates with better outcomes |

## Step 3: The Strange Element Worth Preserving

**Contributions measured by proportional sacrifice, not absolute amount.**

This is counterintuitive but powerful: A gig worker who contributes 3% during a terrible month has demonstrated MORE commitment than a salaried worker auto-deducting 10%. The system should recognize this.

## Step 4: The Translated Design

```json
{
  "label": "Witnessed Sacrifice Retirement",
  "design_principles": "Measure proportional effort not absolute dollars. Public commitment creates binding obligation. Transition readiness matters more than arbitrary age. Variable income is a feature (reveals true sacrifice), not a bug.",
  "core_mechanism": "Track contribution-as-percentage-of-variable-income with public witnessing. Accumulated 'sacrifice ratio' creates claims on pooled resources. Higher sacrifice during hardship = stronger claim.",
  "how_it_works": "1) Banking integration tracks income volatility and contribution percentage in real-time. 2) Each contribution triggers 'witnessed commitment' notification to chosen accountability network. 3) Hardship-adjusted sacrifice score accumulates (contributing 5% when income drops 40% scores higher than 10% at stable income). 4) Collective pool funded by all participants. 5) Withdrawal eligibility based on cumulative sacrifice score + multi-signal readiness assessment (health metrics, burnout indicators, career trajectory, self-reported readiness). 6) Transition includes structured 'role change' period with mentorship obligations and community acknowledgment ceremony.",
  "what_is_new": "Inverts retirement logic: not 'save X dollars' but 'demonstrate proportional commitment.' Variable income becomes advantage—it reveals authentic sacrifice. Community witnessing replaces individual accumulation.",
  "why_it_works": "Variable income workers often sacrifice MORE proportionally than stable earners—current systems punish this. Public commitment dramatically increases follow-through. Pooled risk with sacrifice-weighted claims naturally redistributes from lucky to unlucky without means-testing stigma. Readiness-based timing prevents both premature burnout and artificial delays.",
  "why_it_fails": "Sacrifice scoring could be gamed (fake hardship claims). Requires robust income verification which invades privacy. Public witnessing may shame those who can't contribute. Pooled systems face free-rider problems. 'Readiness assessment' could become arbitrary gatekeeping.",
  "medium_term": "Gig workers and seasonal earners gain retirement access for first time. Community witnessing networks form around workplaces and neighborhoods. 'Sacrifice ratio' becomes respected metric. Phased transitions become norm.",
  "long_term_vision": "Retirement transforms from financial threshold to life-stage transition with community acknowledgment. Income volatility loses its terror—the system recognizes struggle as contribution. Elders carry authority from witnessed sacrifice, not accumulated wealth. The question shifts from 'can I afford to stop?' to 'am I ready to transform, and will my community witness it?'"
}
```

solution.label: Witnessed Sacrifice Retirement
solution.design_principles: Measure proportional effort not absolute dollars. Public commitment creates binding obligation. Transition readiness matters more than arbitrary age. Variable income is a feature (reveals true sacrifice), not a bug.
solution.core_mechanism: Track contribution-as-percentage-of-variable-income with public witnessing. Accumulated 'sacrifice ratio' creates claims on pooled resources. Higher sacrifice during hardship = stronger claim.
solution.how_it_works: 1) Banking integration tracks income volatility and contribution percentage in real-time. 2) Each contribution triggers 'witnessed commitment' notification to chosen accountability network. 3) Hardship-adjusted sacrifice score accumulates (contributing 5% when income drops 40% scores higher than 10% at stable income). 4) Collective pool funded by all participants. 5) Withdrawal eligibility based on cumulative sacrifice score + multi-signal readiness assessment (health metrics, burnout indicators, career trajectory, self-reported readiness). 6) Transition includes structured 'role change' period with mentorship obligations and community acknowledgment ceremony.
solution.what_is_new: Inverts retirement logic: not 'save X dollars' but 'demonstrate proportional commitment.' Variable income becomes advantage—it reveals authentic sacrifice. Community witnessing replaces individual accumulation.
solution.why_it_works: Variable income workers often sacrifice MORE proportionally than stable earners—current systems punish this. Public commitment dramatically increases follow-through. Pooled risk with sacrifice-weighted claims naturally redistributes from lucky to unlucky without means-testing stigma. Readiness-based timing prevents both premature burnout and artificial delays.
solution.why_it_fails: Sacrifice scoring could be gamed (fake hardship claims). Requires robust income verification which invades privacy. Public witnessing may shame those who can't contribute. Pooled systems face free-rider problems. 'Readiness assessment' could become arbitrary gatekeeping.
solution.medium_term: Gig workers and seasonal earners gain retirement access for first time. Community witnessing networks form around workplaces and neighborhoods. 'Sacrifice ratio' becomes respected metric. Phased transitions become norm.
solution.long_term_vision: Retirement transforms from financial threshold to life-stage transition with community acknowledgment. Income volatility loses its terror—the system recognizes struggle as contribution. Elders carry authority from witnessed sacrifice, not accumulated wealth. The question shifts from 'can I afford to stop?' to 'am I ready to transform, and will my community witness it?'
